events.title: EC archives of the week (12-18/07): Brussels European Council (12-13/07/1976, 50 years ago) Birth Simone Veil (13/07/1927, 99 years ago) France National Day (14/07) Terrorist attack in Nice (14/07/2016, 10 years ago) 11th Asia/Europe Meeting (ASEM11), Ulaanbaatar, Mongolia (14-16/07/2016, 10 years ago) European floods (14/07/2021, 5 years ago) Visit of Ursula von der Leyen, President of the European Commission, to Ireland to present the Commission’s assessment of the national recovery plan under NextGenerationEU (16/07/2021, 5 years ago) Election of Simone Veil as President of the EP (17/07/1979, 47 years ago) Nelson Mandela International Day (18/07)

items.title: European Council, in Brussels 12-13/07/1976 and pro-European demonstration in front of the EEC buildings
items.summary: The Heads of State or Government met in Brussels on 12-13/07/1976 for a meeting of the European Council. The Council gave its agreement on the following distribution and number of seats for the European Parliament which will be elected in 1978 by direct universal suffrage: Luxembourg 6; Ireland 15; Denmark 16; Belgium 24; The Netherlands 25; France 81; Italy 81; United Kingdom 81; Germany; 81. Within the European Council, a broad agreement emerged with respect to the appreciation of the economic situation. It was characterised by a general improvement of circumstances. The Council is convinced that this recovery will continue in 1977. However, relatively high unemployment rates should still be expected for a fairly long period of time. The progression of these rates will continue to be monitored particularly closely in certain Member States.

items.title: Memoclip: Tribute to Simone Veil
items.summary: This memoclip illustrates the relations between Simone Veil and the European institutions, from her election as President of the European Parliament in 1979 until the inauguration of the Simone Veil Agora in Brussels in 2011. Simone Veil was the first President of the European Parliament elected by universal suffrage, from 1979 to 1982.

items.title: General views of Paris (France) and official buildings
items.summary: This Infoclip presents a series of general views of Paris, covering major landmarks such as the Champs-Élysées, the Panthéon, the Seine and Place de la Concorde. Footage features several national institutions, including the Ministry for Europe and Foreign Affairs and the Senate, located within the historic Palais du Luxembourg. Additional views show the Élysée Palace, the headquarters of Medef, the leading French employers’ organisation, and the Hôtel de Ville, seat of the Paris city administration. It also includes scenes from the Jardin du Luxembourg and the Jardin des Invalides.

items.title: European Union Capitals: Paris, France
items.summary: This stockshot provides illustrative images of Paris, capital of France, filmed in November 2020. It presents general views of the city, official buildings and sights, general atmosphere in the streets and historic places and buildings in the heart of the city during the coronavirus pandemic.

items.title: Joint press statement by Donald Tusk, President of the European Council, and Jean-Claude Juncker, President of the EC, following the Nice attack
items.summary: On 15 July 2016, Jean-Claude Juncker, President of the EC, and Donald Tusk, President of the European Council, gave a joint press conference at the 11th ASEM Summit held in Mongolia following the Nice attack.

items.title: 11th Asia/Europe Meeting (ASEM11), Ulaanbaatar, Mongolia, 14-16/07/2016
items.summary: The 11th Asia/Europe Meeting – the ASEM Summit – took place on 15 and 16 July 2016 in Ulaanbaatar, Mongolia, under the theme "20 Years of ASEM: Partnership for the Future through Connectivity". The Asia-Europe Meeting (ASEM) is an informal process of dialogue and cooperation bringing together the EU and its Member States, plus Switzerland and Norway, with 21 Asian countries and the Secretariat of the Association of Southeast Asian Nations (ASEAN). Jean-Claude Juncker, President of the EC, and Donald Tusk, President of the European Council, accompanied by Federica Mogherini, High Representative of the Union for Foreign Affairs and Security Policy and Vice-President of the EC, represented the EU at ASEM11. Leaders discussed the future direction of the Asia/Europe Meeting as a platform for inter-regional dialogue; economic cooperation and connectivity; and global and regional challenges to international stability and sustainable development, terrorism, climate change and migration.

items.title: Visit of Ursula von der Leyen, President of the European Commission, Janez Lenarčič and Didier Reynders, European Commissioners, and Alexander De Croo, Belgian Prime Minister, to floodings affected region in Belgium
items.summary: On 17 July 2021, Ursula von der Leyen, President of the European Commission, Janez Lenarčič, European Commissioner for Crisis Management, and Didier Reynders, European Commissioner for Justice, went to floodings affected region in Belgium (Jemelle, Rochefort and Pepinster) to support citizens. Along with Alexander De Croo, Belgian Prime Minister, Pierre-Yves Dermagne, Belgian Deputy Prime Minister and Minister for Economy and Labour, and former Mayor of Rochefort, and Philippe Godin, Mayor of Pepinster. This video shows the visit and the statement given by Ursula von der Leyen on this occasion.

items.title: Floods in Belgium, July 2021
items.summary: This video stockshot shows illustration images of the serious flooding that hit the regions of Verviers and Liège in Belgium around 14 July 2021.

items.title: Floods in Belgium, July 2021 (part 2)
items.summary: This video stockshot shows illustration images of the serious flooding that hit Belgium in July 2021. Images show the towns of Jemelle, Rochefort and Pepinster, filmed on 17 July 2021.

items.title: Visit of Ursula von der Leyen, President of the European Commission, to Ireland to present the Commission’s assessment of the national recovery plan under NextGenerationEU: arrival, welcome, tour of the campus, bilateral meeting and handing over of the Commission decision
items.summary: On 16 July 2021, Ursula von der Leyen, President of the European Commission, and Mairead McGuinness, European Commissioner for Financial Services, Financial Stability, and Capital Markets Union, went in Ireland, where they met with Micheál Martin, Irish Prime Minister (Taoiseach), and David FitzPatrick, President of Technological University Dublin.Ursula von der Leyen and Micheál Martin presented the assessment of the European Commission of the national recovery and resilience plan under NextGenerationEU. This financing of €989 million in grants and loans, under the Recovery and Resilience Facility (RRF), has been set to support the implementation of the crucial investment and reform measures outlined in recovery and resilience plan of Ireland and should play a key role in enabling Ireland to emerge stronger from the COVID-19 pandemic. This event was held at Technological University Dublin.

items.title: Election of Simone Veil as President of the EP; plenary session of the EP
items.summary: Simone Veil was elected President of the EP during this plenary session, the first session after the EP was elected by direct universal suffrage

items.title: Nelson Mandela and the European institutions
items.summary: Nelson Mandela is one of the world's most revered statesmen. He led the struggle to replace the apartheid regime of South Africa by a multi-racial democracy. Sentenced to life in prison, he spent 27 years in jail before becoming the country's first black President. He played a leading role in the drive for peace. He is laureate of the 1988 Sakharov Prize and of the 1993 Nobel Peace Prize®. This video illustrates the relations between Nelson Mandela and the European institutions.

items.summary: High-level debate on EU enlargement with Marta KOS, European Commissioner for Enlargement, Mihai POPSOI, Minister of Foreign Affairs of the Republic of Moldova, Zoran DIMITROVSKI, Deputy Minister of Foreign Affairs and Foreign Trade of the Republic of North Macedonia, Nerijus ALEKSIEJŪNAS, Ambassador, Permanent Representative of the Republic of Lithuania to the EU, David McALLISTER, Member of the European Parliament, Chair of the AFET Committee (video-message), and participants from the enlargement candidate members initiative

events.title: Court of Justice of the European Union

items.title: Court of Justice of the European Union
items.summary: ECJ Joined cases in cases C-258/23 Imagens Médicas Integradas S.A., C-259/23 Synlabhealth II S.A., C-260/23 SIBS-Sociedade Gestoras de Paricipaçoes Sociais S.A. regarding infringement of the rules of competition and the right to respect for personal data

items.title: Court of Justice of the European Union
items.summary: ECJ ruling in case C-196/24 Aucrinde regarding cooperation between courts of the member states in the taking of evidence: exhumation of a body for the purpose of establishing parentage

items.title: Court of Justice of the European Union
items.summary: ECJ rulings in cases C-523/24 Sociedad Civil Catalana and C-666/24 Associació Catalana de Víctimes d’Organitzacions Terroristes regarding the compatibility of amnesty Spanish law regarding Catalonia with EU law

items.title: Court of Justice of the European Union
items.summary: ECJ ruling in case C-280/25 Lin II regarding the protection of the financial interests of the European union

items.title: Court of Justice of the European Union
items.summary: ECJ ruling in case C-26/25 Bukla regarding the return directive

items.title: Court of Justice of the European Union
items.summary: ECJ ruling in case C-209/23 RRC Sports regarding the regulations of football agents

items.title: Court of Justice of the European Union
items.summary: ECJ joined cases in C-424/24 FIGC and Coni and C-425/24 FIGC and Coni regarding disciplinary sanction imposed pursuant to sport law

items.title: Court of Justice of the European Union
items.summary: ECJ ruling in case C-421/24 AGCOM regarding advertising on gambling

items.title: Court of Justice of the European Union
items.summary: Opinion of the advocate general in case C-524/24 Italy v Austria regarding the prohibition of the cross-border traffic of certain heavy goods (Brenner motorway
